notes.title: Sergeant Kovac — Day 1
notes.text: First infected sighting at 0340 hours near the market district. We contained the area and called it in. HQ said it was a localized incident — nothing to worry about. By morning, three officers were showing symptoms. We quarantined them in cell block B. I don't think this is localized.

notes.title: Sergeant Kovac — Day 3
notes.text: HQ stopped responding at noon. Half the station has either turned or fled. I locked the armory and barricaded the back entrance. Found a survivor in the holding cells — young woman, uninfected. Sent her toward the hospital with whatever supplies I could spare. Hope she made it. I'm staying here as long as I can.

notes.title: Emergency Broadcast — Printed Copy
notes.text: ATTENTION ALL SURVIVORS. Military evacuation is active. Proceed to rooftop extraction points in your district. Bring only essentials. Do NOT approach infected individuals. Extraction windows are limited. This message will repeat every six hours. Stay calm. Help is coming.

notes.title: Wall Message
notes.text: Day 5. Streets are clear of people but not of them. I've been moving at night — they seem slower in the cold. Heading for the metro. If you're reading this, check the patrol car near the intersection. There's a flashlight in the glove box. You'll need it. — R

notes.title: Torn Diary Page
notes.text: I saw what they did to Marcus. He was fine yesterday. This morning he was... different. Eyes wrong. Moving wrong. I ran. I don't know if I'm infected. I don't feel sick. Yet. The hospital must have answers. It has to.

notes.title: Crumpled Leaflet
notes.text: THEY KNEW. Project APEX was running under the city for THREE YEARS. Volunteers who signed up for 'medical trials' never came back. The lab is real. The outbreak wasn't an accident. Find the lab. Find the truth. Burn it down. — The Informed

notes.title: Metro Maintenance Log
notes.text: Entry 47. Air recyclers in tunnel C are offline again. Put in the maintenance request two weeks ago — no response. The smell down here is getting worse. Some of the staff are refusing to work the night shift. Can't blame them. Something's been moving in the far tunnels. Probably rats. Probably.

notes.title: Note Taped to a Locker
notes.text: If you're looking for shelter — don't stay here. They came through the tunnels three days ago. Fast ones. We lost six people in the first hour. I made it to the maintenance room. There's a gas mask in the equipment locker — take it. You'll need it upstairs. The hospital has supplies but the air there is bad. Don't go without protection.

notes.title: Hospital Intake Form — Redacted
notes.text: Patient ID: [REDACTED]. Admitted: Day 0, 0210 hrs. Status: Critical. Symptoms: Elevated aggression, rapid tissue mutation, loss of language function. Origin: Underground Laboratory, Level 3. Transfer authorized by: Dr. Reeves. Note: Patient escaped containment during transfer. Location unknown. Notify security immediately.

notes.title: Nurse Okafor — Personal Log
notes.text: We've stopped pretending this is treatable. The infection rewrites something fundamental. Not just biology — behavior, recognition, memory. The patients don't know us anymore. Dr. Reeves says there's a suppression formula — something that slows the process. He went to the lab to finalize it. That was two days ago. I haven't heard from him since.

notes.title: Research Summary — Infection Stages
notes.text:
Stage 1 (0-25%): Mild fever, heightened adrenaline. Subject appears functional.
Stage 2 (25-50%): Cognitive deterioration begins. Emotional regulation impaired.
Stage 3 (50-75%): Aggressive behavior, partial loss of identity. Physical enhancement noted.
Stage 4 (75-100%): Full transformation imminent. Subject is no longer recoverable.

Suppression serums can delay progression but cannot reverse it. Formula attached separately.

notes.title: Project APEX — Internal Briefing
notes.text: Project APEX objectives: Develop a self-replicating biological agent capable of selective host modification. Military applications: enhanced soldiers, controlled aggression, reduced fear response. Civilian applications: classified. Current status: Phase 3 trials complete. Alpha strain shows unexpected autonomous behavior. Recommend immediate containment review. Funding approved for Phase 4.

notes.title: Dr. Reeves — Last Entry
notes.text:
I created this. I have to say that. Not the army, not the board — me. I thought we were building something that would save lives. I was wrong about everything. The Alpha strain evolved beyond our models. It's learning. It recognizes threats. It adapts.

The suppression formula is on Terminal 3. It won't cure you. Nothing will. But it will buy time. Get to the rooftop. The extraction signal is still active. Someone has to make it out. Someone has to tell them what happened here.

notes.title: Security Alert — Level 5
notes.text:
CONTAINMENT BREACH — ALPHA SPECIMEN
Time: 0318
Location: Level 3 Corridor
Status: CRITICAL

Alpha specimen has breached all containment measures. All personnel are ordered to evacuate via rooftop extraction point. Do NOT engage. Lethal force has proven ineffective. Specimen appears to have developed tactical awareness. Repeat: DO NOT ENGAGE.

notes.title: Intercepted Radio Transmission
notes.text:
[Recording — partial]
'...extraction window closes at dawn. If anyone is still alive in the zone... the pilot won't wait... something on the roof is blocking the approach... we're seeing movement on thermal... God, what IS that...' [Signal lost]
